entries.name: Sydney Tower Eye
entries.desc: The golden-topped tower that is Sydney's tallest structure, in the central business district. Its deck and open-air Skywalk overlook the harbor, Opera House, and Harbour Bridge.

photo.alt: Sydney Tower rising above the city skyline
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sydney-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Sydney Tower 4.jpg" by Alex Proimos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sydney_Tower_4.jpg -->

entries.name: Eureka Skydeck
entries.desc: The observation deck near the top of the Eureka Tower in Southbank, overlooking the Yarra River and Melbourne. Its glass cube, The Edge, slides out beyond the building's face.

photo.alt: Eureka Tower viewed from Southbank, Melbourne
photo.credit: <!-- photo: eureka-tower-melbourne.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Eureka Tower From Southbank, Melbourne Australia" by Rexness · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Eureka_Tower_From_Southbank,_Melbourne_Australia_(4527851986).jpg -->

entries.name: Mirante do Vale (Edifício Itália)
entries.desc: One of the tallest buildings in the historic center of São Paulo, with a rooftop terrace long open to visitors. It overlooks the seemingly endless expanse of the city's high-rises.

photo.alt: Edifício Itália skyscraper in downtown São Paulo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: edificio-italia-sao-paulo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Edifício Itália, SP.jpg" by Dornicke (Lucas B. Salles) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Edif%C3%ADcio_It%C3%A1lia,_SP.jpg -->

meta: One of São Paulo's Tallest Buildings

entries.name: Sugarloaf Mountain Cable Car
entries.desc: A granite peak at the mouth of Guanabara Bay, reached by a historic two-stage cable car. Its summit overlooks Rio's beaches, harbor, and Corcovado's Christ the Redeemer statue.

photo.alt: Sugarloaf Mountain at the mouth of Guanabara Bay, Rio de Janeiro
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sugarloaf-mountain-rio.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Sugarloaf Mountain, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.jpg" by Wilfredor · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sugarloaf_Mountain,_Rio_de_Janeiro,_Brazil.jpg -->

entries.name: Christ the Redeemer (Corcovado)
entries.desc: The colossal Art Deco statue of Christ atop Corcovado mountain, reached by a cog railway through the Tijuca forest. Its terraces overlook Rio, its bay, and Sugarloaf Mountain.

photo.alt: Christ the Redeemer statue atop Corcovado mountain, Rio de Janeiro
photo.credit: <!-- photo: christ-the-redeemer-corcovado.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 de · "Christ the Redeemer - Cristo Redentor.jpg" by Arne Müseler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Christ_the_Redeemer_-_Cristo_Redentor.jpg -->

entries.name: CN Tower
entries.desc: A concrete communications and observation tower that defines the Toronto skyline. Its decks include a glass floor, and the EdgeWalk lets visitors circle the outside, overlooking Lake Ontario.

photo.alt: CN Tower on the Toronto skyline
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cn-tower-toronto.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Toronto's CN Tower.jpg" by Bill Wrigley · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Toronto%27s_CN_Tower.jpg -->

entries.name: Sky Costanera
entries.desc: The observation deck atop the Gran Torre Santiago, the tallest building in South America. Its 360-degree views take in the Santiago sprawl framed by the snow-capped Andes.

photo.alt: Gran Torre Santiago at the Costanera Center
photo.credit: <!-- photo: gran-torre-santiago.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Gran Torre Santiago, Costanera Center.jpg" by Deensel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gran_Torre_Santiago,_Costanera_Center_(24847266437).jpg -->

entries.name: Top of Shanghai Observatory
entries.desc: Occupying the upper floors of the twisting Shanghai Tower, one of the world's tallest buildings, the deck overlooks the Lujiazui financial district and the Huangpu River bend below.

photo.alt: Shanghai Tower rising above the Lujiazui skyline
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shanghai-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Shanghai - Shanghai Tower - 0003.jpg" by Stefan Fussan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shanghai_-_Shanghai_Tower_-_0003.jpg -->

entries.name: Oriental Pearl Tower
entries.desc: A distinctive TV tower of stacked spheres in Pudong, a symbol of Shanghai. Its observation levels, including a glass-floored deck, look across the Bund and the Huangpu River.

photo.alt: Oriental Pearl Tower's stacked spheres rising over Pudong, Shanghai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: oriental-pearl-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Shanghai Oriental Pearl Tower-20150516-RM-130617.jpg" by Ermell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shanghai_Oriental_Pearl_Tower-20150516-RM-130617.jpg -->

entries.name: Sky100 Hong Kong Observation Deck
entries.desc: An indoor deck near the top of the International Commerce Centre in West Kowloon, offering a 360-degree panorama of Victoria Harbour and the towers of Hong Kong Island opposite.

photo.alt: The International Commerce Centre tower in West Kowloon, Hong Kong, home to the Sky100 deck
photo.credit: <!-- photo: international-commerce-centre.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Hong Kong International Commerce Centre (15512862984).jpg" by Bernard Spragg. NZ · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hong_Kong_International_Commerce_Centre_(15512862984).jpg -->

entries.name: Victoria Peak (The Peak Tower)
entries.desc: The highest hill on Hong Kong Island, reached by the historic Peak Tram. Its terraces frame the classic view over the skyscrapers, Victoria Harbour, and Kowloon beyond.

photo.alt: The historic Peak Tram approaching the Peak Tower on Victoria Peak, Hong Kong
photo.credit: <!-- photo: victoria-peak-tram.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Victoria Peak, Hong Kong.jpg" by Christopher · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Victoria_Peak,_Hong_Kong.jpg -->

entries.name: Canton Tower Observation Deck
entries.desc: A slender, twisting observation and telecommunications tower on the Pearl River, its outdoor decks and open-air rides look out over the Guangzhou skyline and the river below.

photo.alt: The slender, twisting Canton Tower on the Pearl River, Guangzhou
photo.credit: <!-- photo: canton-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Canton Tower.jpg" by Chinatravelsavvy · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Canton_Tower.jpg -->

entries.name: Macau Tower
entries.desc: A convention and observation tower overlooking the Pearl River Delta, with glass-floor sections. It is known for its adventure attractions, including one of the world's highest bungee jumps.

photo.alt: Macau Tower rising above the Pearl River Delta
photo.credit: <!-- photo: macau-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Macau Tower.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Macau_Tower.JPG -->

entries.name: Eiffel Tower
entries.desc: The wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars, built for the 1889 World's Fair and the emblem of Paris. Its upper platform surveys the Seine and the whole of the city.

photo.alt: The Eiffel Tower seen from the Champ de Mars, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: eiffel-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Tour Eiffel Wikimedia Commons.jpg" by Benh LIEU SONG · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tour_Eiffel_Wikimedia_Commons.jpg -->

entries.name: Tour Montparnasse Observation Deck
entries.desc: A lone dark skyscraper on the Left Bank whose rooftop terrace is prized because its view includes the Eiffel Tower, unlike the Eiffel's own decks. It surveys the Paris rooftops in every direction.

photo.alt: The rooftop observation deck and helipad atop Tour Montparnasse, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tour-montparnasse-rooftop.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Tour Montparnasse Roof.jpg" by Paul Sparkes · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tour_Montparnasse_Roof.jpg -->

entries.name: Berliner Fernsehturm
entries.desc: The television tower at Alexanderplatz, Germany's tallest structure, built in the former East Berlin. Its sphere holds an observation deck and revolving restaurant above the whole city.

photo.alt: The Berliner Fernsehturm TV tower at Alexanderplatz, Berlin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: berliner-fernsehturm.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Fernsehturm Berlin Alex.JPG" by Arne Hückelheim · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fernsehturm_Berlin_Alex.JPG -->

entries.name: Hallgrímskirkja Tower
entries.desc: The tower of Reykjavik's tallest church, whose basalt-inspired form dominates the skyline. A lift carries visitors up for a view over the colorful rooftops, harbor, and surrounding mountains.

photo.alt: Hallgrímskirkja church tower illuminated at night, with the statue of Leif Erikson in front
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hallgrimskirkja-church.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Hallgrímskirkja (16282067598)" by sergejf · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hallgr%C3%ADmskirkja_(16282067598).jpg -->

entries.name: Duomo di Milano Rooftop Terraces
entries.desc: The rooftop of Milan's Gothic cathedral, reached by stairs or lift. Visitors walk among the marble spires and flying buttresses with views over the Piazza del Duomo and the city.

photo.alt: Marble spires and Gothic pinnacles on the rooftop of Milan Cathedral
photo.credit: <!-- photo: duomo-di-milano-rooftop.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Duomo di Milano (Milan Cathedral) Rooftop" by Sarah.samoraj · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Duomo_di_Milano_(Milan_Cathedral)_Rooftop.jpg -->

entries.name: Tokyo Skytree Tembo Deck & Tembo Galleria
entries.desc: A broadcasting and observation tower in the Sumida district, among the tallest structures in the world. Its two decks survey the vast Tokyo sprawl, with Mount Fuji visible on clear days.

photo.alt: Tokyo Skytree broadcasting tower rising above the Sumida district skyline
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tokyo-skytree.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Tokyo Sky Tree 2012" by Kakidai · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tokyo_Sky_Tree_2012.JPG -->

entries.name: Tokyo Tower Main & Top Deck
entries.desc: A lattice tower in Minato modeled on the Eiffel Tower and painted orange and white. Its two decks overlook central Tokyo, with the Skytree and Mount Fuji visible in clear weather.

photo.alt: Tokyo Tower's orange-and-white lattice structure seen from below
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tokyo-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Tokyo Tower, Tokyo, 20240821 1724 5273" by Jakub Hałun · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tokyo_Tower,_Tokyo,_20240821_1724_5273.jpg -->

entries.name: Abeno Harukas 300
entries.desc: Set in one of Japan's tallest skyscrapers above Tennoji, the three-story glass observatory wraps the top of the building, overlooking Osaka Bay and, on clear days, distant Awaji Island.

photo.alt: The open-air Sky Garden atrium at the top of Abeno Harukas
photo.credit: <!-- photo: abeno-harukas-sky-garden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Abeno Harukas SKY GARDEN 300" by Project Kei · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Abeno_Harukas_SKY_GARDEN_300.jpg -->

entries.name: Petronas Towers Observation Deck & Skybridge
entries.desc: The twin stainless-steel towers are Kuala Lumpur's icon. Visitors cross the double-decker skybridge linking the towers, then reach an upper observation deck over the city center.

photo.alt: The Petronas Twin Towers with the double-decker skybridge connecting them
photo.credit: <!-- photo: petronas-twin-towers.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "The Petronas Twin Towers in Kuala Lumpur (Malaysia)" by Dudva · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Petronas_Twin_Towers_in_Kuala_Lumpur_(Malaysia).JPG -->

entries.name: KL Tower Observation Deck
entries.desc: A telecommunications tower rising from a forested hill, giving it added height over the city. Its decks and a glass Sky Box look across Kuala Lumpur, including the nearby Petronas Towers.

photo.alt: Kuala Lumpur Tower rising above the city skyline at dusk
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kl-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Menara Kuala Lumpur (Kuala Lumpur Tower) (18972745702)" by Jorge Láscar · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Menara_Kuala_Lumpur_(Kuala_Lumpur_Tower)_(18972745702).jpg -->

entries.name: Torre Latinoamericana
entries.desc: A mid-century skyscraper in the historic center, famous for surviving major earthquakes on soft lakebed soil. Its open-air deck overlooks the vast basin of Mexico City.

photo.alt: Torre Latinoamericana skyscraper viewed from street level against the sky
photo.credit: <!-- photo: torre-latinoamericana.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "La Torre Latinoamericana, Ciudad de México" by Pequeño mar · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:La_Torre_Latinoamericana,_Ciudad_de_M%C3%A9xico.jpg -->

entries.name: A'DAM Lookout
entries.desc: An observation deck atop the A'DAM Toren across the IJ waterway from Amsterdam's center. It overlooks the historic city and harbor and features a swing that hangs out over the roof edge.

photo.alt: A'DAM Toren's rooftop swing hanging over the edge above Amsterdam
photo.credit: <!-- photo: adam-toren-amsterdam.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "A'DAM Toren schommel.jpg" by Marion Golsteijn · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A%27DAM_Toren_schommel.jpg -->

entries.name: Sky Tower
entries.desc: A telecommunications and observation tower that is the tallest structure in New Zealand. Its decks, with glass floor panels, overlook Auckland's harbor, and visitors can walk or jump from the top.

photo.alt: Sky Tower rising above central Auckland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sky-tower-auckland.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Sky Tower from the base, Auckland, New Zealand.jpg" by Jonathan Harker · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sky_Tower_from_the_base,_Auckland,_New_Zealand.jpg -->

entries.name: Miradouro de Santa Luzia & Portas do Sol
entries.desc: Two adjacent terraced viewpoints in the Alfama district, decorated with tiled panels and pergolas. They overlook the maze of old rooftops descending to the Tagus River.

photo.alt: Miradouro de Santa Luzia terrace overlooking Alfama's rooftops
photo.credit: <!-- photo: miradouro-santa-luzia-lisbon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Miradouro de Santa Luzia.jpg" by GiacomoAntonini1760 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Miradouro_de_Santa_Luzia.jpg -->

entries.name: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark
entries.desc: The boat-shaped deck spanning the three towers of Marina Bay Sands. It overlooks the bay, the central business district, and Gardens by the Bay from its cantilevered observation platform.

photo.alt: Marina Bay Sands and its SkyPark spanning the three towers
photo.credit: <!-- photo: marina-bay-sands-singapore.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Marina Bay Sands, Singapore 1.jpg" by Ray in Manila · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Marina_Bay_Sands,_Singapore_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Table Mountain Aerial Cableway
entries.desc: A rotating cable car climbs the flat-topped mountain overlooking Cape Town. The summit gives 360-degree views of the city, Table Bay, and the peninsula down to the Cape of Good Hope.

photo.alt: Table Mountain cableway station below the flat-topped peak
photo.credit: <!-- photo: table-mountain-cableway-cape-town.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Table Mountain cableway, Cape Town (P1050216).jpg" by Matti Blume · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Table_Mountain_cableway,_Cape_Town_(P1050216).jpg -->

entries.name: Lotte World Tower Seoul Sky
entries.desc: Perched atop South Korea's tallest building in the Jamsil district, the deck features glass-floor sections and sweeping views across Seoul and the Han River.

photo.alt: Lotte World Tower against Seoul's evening skyline
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lotte-world-tower-seoul.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Purple sky of Seoul and Lotte World Tower.jpg" by dlducks123 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Purple_sky_of_Seoul_and_Lotte_World_Tower.jpg -->

entries.name: N Seoul Tower (Namsan Tower)
entries.desc: A communication and observation tower atop Namsan hill, reached by cable car. Its deck gives a full panorama of Seoul, and the base is known for its railings of love padlocks.

photo.alt: N Seoul Tower atop Namsan hill
photo.credit: <!-- photo: n-seoul-tower-namsan-seoul.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "N-Seoul-Tower and Namsan Park (26876783888).jpg" by FLASHPACKER TRAVELGUIDE · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:N-Seoul-Tower_and_Namsan_Park_(26876783888).jpg -->

entries.name: Taipei 101 Observatory
entries.desc: Set in the landmark bamboo-shaped skyscraper that was once the world's tallest, the observatory overlooks the Taipei basin and houses the giant tuned mass damper that steadies the tower.

photo.alt: Taipei 101's bamboo-shaped skyscraper rising above the city
photo.credit: <!-- photo: taipei-101-taipei.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Taipei Taiwan Taipei-101-Tower-01.jpg" by CEphoto, Uwe Aranas · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Taipei_Taiwan_Taipei-101-Tower-01.jpg -->
